negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing skip-on-missing origin hash behavior. The port attaches an origin hash from Scheduler storage, but read_origin_hash falls back to B256::ZERO on storage read failure, so transaction creation is not skipped when origin hash is missing/unreadable.
related_but_insufficient_code: create_scheduled_execution_transaction_from_state reads origin_hash and passes it into TxScheduled construction.

rationale: The July 2025 candidates introduced default-zero fallbacks, but the January 2026 candidates explicitly supersede that behavior with skip-on-missing semantics and use of Call::origin_hash.
statement: Canonical scheduling must attach the stored origin hash to each fired scheduled transaction and skip transaction creation when the origin hash is missing or unreadable; the earlier B256::default fallback is obsolete.
suggested_falsification_tests: Make originHash storage unreadable/missing for an otherwise due call and assert no scheduled transaction is created; current helper would use B256::ZERO.
summary: Stored origin hash is attached, but missing/unreadable origin hash does not skip firing.
title: Canonical scheduled firing requires real stored origin hashes
